findings.description: Footer text is moved to the top of the document, interrupting the header reading sequence.
findings.evidence: The footer text 'State of North Carolina...' from the original PDF is placed immediately after the logo in the HTML, before the remaining header information ('PAT MCCRORY Governor', etc.).

findings.description: A decorative footer background graphic is incorrectly inserted as inline content.
findings.evidence: The green-to-blue gradient bar from the bottom of PDF page 1 appears in the HTML between the document outline and the 'I. History/Background' heading.

findings.description: A page number from the original document footer is embedded inline, interrupting a sentence.
findings.evidence: The text 'Page 2 of 5' is inserted directly between the words 'Speakers were' and 'given five minutes'.

findings.description: A page number from the original document footer is embedded inline between content blocks.
findings.evidence: The text 'Page 3 of 5' appears between the end of section II and the heading for section III.

findings.description: A page number from the original document footer is embedded inline, interrupting a sentence.
findings.evidence: The text 'Page 4 of 5' is inserted directly between the word 'One' and the phrase 'comment suggested that...'.

findings.description: A bullet point sentence is split into two separate list items due to a page break, disrupting the text flow.
findings.evidence:
In the PDF, the 'Groundwater Assessments' bullet point continues across pages 4 and 5 ('...around the Lee facility. One comment suggested...'). In the HTML, this is broken into two separate list items: `
  • ...around the Lee facility. One
    • ` on page 4 and `
  • comment suggested...
    • ` on page 5.
